Scenario.overview: Communications Simple Briefly, the attackers compromised the Oldsmar Water Treatment Plant using spear phishing, followed by exploitation of remote services and custom command and control protocols to establish persistence and impact the plant's operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Network Traffic Target Artifact (Protocols) File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) Custom Command and Control Protocol File Metadata (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-38180 (Suggested): Microsoft.NET Core and Visual Studio contain an unspecified vulnerability that allows for denial-of-service (DoS).
stage.description: The attackers sent a spear-phishing email to an employee, exploiting their email account to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2021-27102 (Suggested): Accellion FTA contains an OS command injection vulnerability exploited via a local web service call.
stage.description: The attackers used remote services like TeamViewer or AnyDesk to gain control over the targeted system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers modified setpoints for a critical chemical in the water treatment process.

Scenario.summary: The attackers successfully compromised the Oldsmar Water Treatment Plant using a combination of spear phishing, remote services exploitation, and supply chain compromise, ultimately impacting the plant's operations.
